cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: CiteLens Study: SEO Drives AI Citations on Google and Perplexity, But Not ChatGPT
stories.plain: A benchmark across 320 queries finds Google AI Mode and Perplexity follow traditional SEO rankings roughly 90% of the time, while ChatGPT ignores search rank and brand size entirely. This splits GEO strategy in two: what earns a citation in one AI engine may not work in another, forcing marketers to rethink a one-size-fits-all approach to AI visibility.

stories.head: Generative Engine Optimization Goes Mainstream: The 2026 AI-Visibility Landscape
stories.plain: A landscape report maps the explosion of GEO platforms built to help brands appear inside AI-generated answers instead of traditional search results. As buyer journeys increasingly start and end inside AI assistants, this category now shapes how marketing budgets and content strategies are allocated — making it essential reading for anyone buying or building martech.

stories.head: Pega's Don Schuerman on the Gap Between AI Ambition and Actual Marketing Results
stories.plain: Pega's chief technology strategist argues that the reason most AI pilots fail to produce CFO-pleasing results is not the technology — it's that AI is being layered onto processes built for a pre-AI era. The conversation covers agentic decisioning, governance gaps, and what it actually takes to turn AI spend into measurable performance, making it directly useful for marketers holding a growing AI invoice.

stories.head: Multiply Launches Self-Learning ABM Advertising That Optimises Per Account
stories.plain: Multiply's new product lets marketers spin up personalised account-based ad campaigns in ten minutes, then continuously improves performance across every target account using self-learning algorithms. Applying bandit-style optimisation to ABM advertising — traditionally a slow, manual process — is a meaningful change in how B2B media is bought and measured.

stories.head: Infobip Acquires SocketLabs to Strengthen Enterprise Email Infrastructure and Routing
stories.plain: Infobip buys SocketLabs, gaining nearly two decades of high-volume email expertise, vendor-agnostic deliverability observability, and intelligent multi-provider routing. For enterprise marketers, this consolidation matters because reliable email infrastructure underpins every downstream personalisation and engagement strategy — and fewer independent infrastructure vendors means less choice in the stack.

stories.head: CiteLens Study: SEO Drives AI Citations on Google and Perplexity, But Not ChatGPT
stories.plain: A benchmark across 320 queries found Google AI Mode and Perplexity follow traditional SEO rankings ~90% of the time, while ChatGPT ignores both rankings and brand size entirely. This fractures the idea of a single AI-SEO playbook and forces marketers to build platform-specific visibility strategies — a fundamental shift in how brands buy and measure discoverability.

stories.head: Generative Engine Optimization Goes Mainstream: The 2026 AI-Visibility Landscape
stories.plain: A category overview of the exploding GEO vendor landscape as buyers shift from search engines to AI assistants for answers. It maps who the players are, what they sell, and how brands should budget for AI-answer visibility — directly relevant to anyone deciding where to spend on discoverability in 2026.

stories.head: The CMS Is Becoming the AI Operating System for Brands
stories.plain: This analysis argues that choosing a CMS now means choosing your AI infrastructure — covering discovery, personalization, governance, and agentic workflows all in one platform decision. It reframes a familiar purchase as a high-stakes strategic bet, which matters for anyone evaluating or renewing content technology.

stories.head: Infobip Acquires SocketLabs to Expand Enterprise Email Infrastructure
stories.plain: Infobip is buying SocketLabs, a specialist in high-volume multi-provider email routing and deliverability observability. The deal consolidates email infrastructure under an AI-first communications platform, signaling that reliable data pipes — not just smart models — are becoming a competitive moat in enterprise engagement stacks.

stories.head: Typeface Expands Ad and Video Agents, Previews Closed-Loop Optimization
stories.plain: Typeface upgraded its Ad and Video agents and is previewing closed-loop optimization — where the system measures ad performance and automatically feeds results back to improve future creative. This moves generative content tools from output machines toward self-improving decisioning systems, changing how teams evaluate and buy creative AI.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI takes operational control of marketing workflows
body: AI agents are no longer demo-ware — they are being embedded directly into production marketing systems. From Cresta's simulator training human reps with AI counterparts, to IBM's multi-agent software development workflows, to Globant & Vercel shipping 'AI Pods' for enterprise deployment, the pattern is the same: autonomous agents handling end-to-end tasks, not just assisting humans.

The strategic decision this forces is about where humans stay in the loop. Pega's Don Schuerman warns of the gap between AI ambition and reality, and HubSpot's Breeze expansion shows even mainstream CRMs racing to make agents the default mode — meaning teams that haven't defined human-agent boundaries are already behind.
ev: Agile Brand · Globant & Vercel AI Pods
Agile Brand · Pega AI ambition vs reality

themes.head: Generative Engine Optimization: AI visibility replaces SEO
body: The CiteLens study and the GEO mainstream report together deliver a concrete finding: SEO signals still govern AI citations on Google and Perplexity, but ChatGPT operates by different rules entirely — meaning brands need platform-specific visibility strategies, not a single SEO playbook. MarTech's 'AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure' confirms that legacy traffic and ranking KPIs are now structurally misleading.

The practical shift is that trust signals — citations, authority, structured data — become the new PageRank. MarTech's piece on trust as the primary visibility signal in AI search frames this as a brand governance problem as much as a technical one. Teams still optimising for click-through rates are measuring a channel that AI-mediated discovery is quietly bypassing.
ev: MarTech Series · CiteLens SEO & AI citations

themes.head: The CMS evolves into an AI operating system
body: Content management platforms are being repositioned as the central orchestration layer for AI-generated and AI-distributed content. TIME's partnership with Contentstack and MarTech's direct argument that 'the CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands' point to the same structural change: the content repository is now also the AI context store, personalisation engine, and distribution hub.

This matters for budget decisions because it collapses what were separate martech categories — DAM, CMS, personalisation, CDP — into a single platform conversation. Vendors that can credibly claim the 'operating system' position will capture disproportionate stack spend, while point solutions that don't integrate at the content layer risk being displaced.

themes.head: GTM efficiency crisis: rising CAC, wrong AI bets
body: Two MarTech opinion pieces land a pointed critique: most teams are using AI to produce more top-of-funnel content and outreach — precisely the activities that inflate CAC without improving deal quality. The companion piece on rising customer acquisition costs argues the problem is structural misalignment between AI deployment and revenue-generating workflow steps.

The corrective framing is that AI should be applied where conversion friction actually lives — qualification, deal progression, retention — not just content generation at scale. The hidden cost of fragmented martech stacks compounds this: more tools generating more signals that nobody converts into better decisions, which is the diagnostic problem Qualtrics is betting its data-edge thesis against.
ev: MarTech · AI scaling wrong GTM part
MarTech · Hidden cost of fragmented stack

themes.head: Self-learning, closed-loop advertising goes live
body: Multiply's 10-minute ABM setup with self-learning ad targeting and Typeface's closed-loop optimisation preview signal that the 'set and forget' ad system is arriving in production, not just in pitch decks. These platforms ingest engagement signals, retrain targeting models, and adjust creative — all without a human campaign manager in the loop for routine iterations.

For marketing leaders this compresses the competitive window on paid media: advantage will come from the quality of first-party data and audience definitions fed in, not from manual optimisation skill. SeoSamba's LocalEko, which runs local marketing 'in the background' for agencies, shows the same closed-loop logic spreading down-market to SMB — meaning self-learning ad systems will soon be table stakes across tiers.
ev: MarTech Series · SeoSamba LocalEko background marketing

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack — content, search, ads, GTM, and agents — the decisive variable is no longer which AI tool you use but whether your data, trust signals, and workflow design are good enough to make autonomous systems perform better than your competitors'.
